In addition to the efficient use of energy, renewable energy sources are considered the most important pillar of a sustainable energy policy and the energy transition.

Traditional biomass, used for cooking and heating in developing countries, accounted for the largest share of 6.9 %, followed by modern biomass and solar and geothermal energy (4.3%), hydroelectric power (3.6%), other modern renewable energies, especially wind and photovoltaics (together 2.1%) and biofuels (1 %).
Change in the energy system The transformation from conventional energy supply to renewable energy changes the structure of the energy industry massively.

Energy storage The greater the share of renewable energies, the greater the importance of storage possibilities to align the fluctuations in energy production with the fluctuations in energy consumption and thus create security of supply.
Intelligent electricity consumption The construction of smart grids plays an important role in the conversion of electricity supply.

In addition to direct emissions, the climate balance and the consumption of resources, construction and dismantling of the plants (goods life cycle), production, operation, disposal etc. must be considered for a holistic view.
